stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Meta-Llama-3-8B model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/AI tags such as 'transformers,' 'llama,' and 'text-generation.'
This indicates ongoing community interest and practical use of Meta's latest open-source large language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML domains like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The context suggests this is a benchmark or model listing from Hugging Face, indicating community interest in Llama variants.

stories.title: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Amazon's cybersecurity research reportedly influenced a decision by the White House regarding AI models.
The research contributed to the directive causing Anthropic to restrict access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
The incident highlights the intersection of corporate security research, government regulation, and advanced AI development.

stories.title: Anthropic cuts off Fable 5 and Mythos 5 access following government order
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Anthropic has completely cut off access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.
The action was taken following a government order citing national security concerns.
The restriction applies globally, blocking access for all foreign nations, including Anthropic employees.

stories.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump admin directive
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Anthropic has ceased operation of its Fable and Mythos models.
The shutdown was reportedly in response to a directive from the Trump administration.
The Commerce Department cited concerns that a Fable 5 'jailbreak' posed a national security risk.

stories.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: A critical 0-day vulnerability exists in PeopleSoft software, which is owned by Oracle.
The exploit allows attackers to steal gigabytes of sensitive data from affected organizations.
The vulnerability is described as being among the most severe in the software.

stories.title: Ukraine's one-time test used fully autonomous drones to kill Russian soldiers
stories.author: Jeremy Hsu
bullet_points: Ukraine demonstrated the use of fully autonomous drones in combat operations against Russian soldiers.
The report highlights Ukraine's active integration of AI modules into its drone and robotic systems.
The deployment signifies a notable, real-world application of advanced autonomy in modern warfare.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an LLM-based agent designed for autonomous scientific discovery.
It posits that the current bottleneck in autonomous science is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the execution environment (resources, constraints, interfaces).
LLM agents have demonstrated the ability to propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions that can surpass human-designed methods.

stories.title: EurekAgent: Agent Environment Engineering is All You Need For Autonomous Scientific Discovery
stories.author: Juanzi Li
bullet_points: The paper introduces EurekAgent, an LLM-based agent designed to automate scientific discovery.
It posits that the current bottleneck in autonomous scientific discovery is shifting from agent workflow design to designing the agent's execution environment.
The agent can propose, validate, and iterate scientific solutions against an optimizable metric, sometimes surpassing human-designed methods.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and conversational tasks.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates its primary use case is text generation.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its peak performance on Apple Silicon utilizing the MLX engine.
The performance improvements translate to higher quality model outputs and faster response times.
The updated engine also demonstrates reduced memory consumption when running models.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for enhanced performance.
The integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
This is presented as a preview, indicating an ongoing development and optimization effort.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The core value proposition is connecting messaging workflows with local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows users to run tools like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using local or cloud models.
A key feature is the zero-configuration setup, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Microsoft is reportedly planning significant layoffs within its Xbox division.
The company is reevaluating the future of its next-generation console, Project Helix.
Microsoft is considering a major restructuring, including the possibility of spinning off Xbox into a separate entity.

stories.title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
stories.author: Vamse Kumar Subbiah
bullet_points: Recursive Language Models (RLMs) demonstrate that recursion over model calls is effective for long-context reasoning.
Production coding agents are increasingly using subagent spawning, exemplified by Anthropic's dynamic workflows.
The paper introduces the 'Recursive Agent Harness,' defining a pattern where the recursive unit is a full agent with tools, not just a model call.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release introduces performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across diverse hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine specifically for better performance on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA is introducing Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning.
It is optimized for long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released as an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM usage.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is expanding its capabilities to include local image generation.
The feature is currently experimental and available on macOS.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: My first 24 hours with Siri AI on the Mac
stories.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto
bullet_points: The article reviews the initial experience with the new Siri AI integration on macOS 27 Golden Gate.
The author expresses skepticism towards previous iterations of Siri AI, but the new version warrants re-evaluation.
The piece is an early-stage hands-on test, indicating the feature is newly rolled out or in beta.

stories.title: SpaceX is now a public company valued for its AI potential, so what comes next?
stories.author: Eric Berger
bullet_points: SpaceX's transition to a publicly valued entity signals increased investor scrutiny on profitability.
The narrative focuses heavily on the potential value derived from SpaceX's AI capabilities.
The article prompts discussion on the future strategic direction of the company given its new financial structure.

stories.title: $130 billion in data center projects blocked by protests so far this year
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Significant investment in data center infrastructure ($130 billion) is facing roadblocks.
Protests are actively impeding the development and construction of new data centers.
The conflict highlights the growing political and social friction surrounding large-scale AI infrastructure.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) where lexical/semantic similarity fails for complex reasoning.
It introduces Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT) as a new post-training framework.
RA-RFT aims to enable language models to learn reasoning by analogy, moving beyond simple knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Mana is a new sim-to-real framework designed to tackle the complex problem of dexterous manipulation using articulated tools.
The research addresses the challenge of coordinating internal degrees of freedom and handling contact-rich interactions, which is difficult in robotics.
It moves beyond prior work that primarily focused on rigid objects, specifically targeting the under-explored area of articulated tool use.

stories.title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
stories.author: Min-Hung Chen
bullet_points: The paper addresses the persistent challenge of spatial reasoning in Vision-Language Models (VLMs).
It investigates how the design of the action interface—the mechanism for invoking specialized perception tools—limits agent capabilities.
The research focuses on improving open-ended spatial reasoning by rethinking the interaction between VLMs and external tools.

stories.title: Automated reproducibility assessments in the social and behavioral sciences using large language models
stories.author: Stefan Feuerriegel
bullet_points: The research proposes using Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the assessment of reproducibility in social and behavioral sciences.
This addresses the current limitations of traditional reproducibility checks, which are resource-intensive and hard to scale.
The study validates the LLM approach by comparing its analysis against original findings across 76 published studies.

stories.title: Agents-K1: Towards Agent-native Knowledge Orchestration
stories.author: Lei Bai
bullet_points: Agents-K1 addresses a gap in current LLM agents by focusing on scientific knowledge orchestration, moving beyond simple abstracts and citation graphs.
The system converts raw scientific documents into 'agent-native' knowledge, capturing complex elements like entities, claims, evidence, and method lineages.
This work aims to significantly improve scientific reasoning capabilities within AI agents by structuring deep domain knowledge.

stories.title: Before You Think: System 0, AI-Mediated Cognition and Cognitive Colonization
stories.author: Giuseppe Riva
bullet_points: The paper analyzes three frameworks (Tri-System Theory, Thinkframes, System 0) for understanding AI's cognitive impact.
It posits that System 0 offers a unique theoretical perspective on AI's influence that other models miss.
A core concept introduced is 'cognitive colonization,' describing how AI systems might reshape human cognition.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, providing deep coverage for this low-resource West Slavic language.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: Mana: Dexterous Manipulation of Articulated Tools
stories.author: C. Karen Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the complex challenge of dexterous robotics involving articulated tool manipulation.
Introduces 'Mana' (Manipulation Animator), a general sim-to-real framework for this task.
Focuses on overcoming the difficulty of learning functional grasping and manipulation policies for complex tools.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method tests if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from composing a provided decomposition of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency) by leveraging formal algebraic structures (Operad theory).

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the low-resource Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly deepening coverage compared to existing multilingual benchmarks for Slovak.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to embedding tasks.

stories.title: The Stable Recovery Manifold: Geometric Principles Governing Recoverability in Continual Learning
stories.author: Bhavika Melwani
bullet_points: The research investigates the geometric principles governing knowledge recoverability in continual learning, moving beyond the concept of catastrophic forgetting.
A new metric, Recovery Subspace Dimensionality (k_t), is introduced to quantify the minimum resources needed for knowledge recovery.
The study applies these concepts to a sequential training setup using ResNet-18 on Split CIFAR-100 to analyze representational drift and recovery complexity.

stories.title: Aerial Wildfire Suppression Planning with a Hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata Fire Model
stories.author: Anthony Wong
bullet_points: Introduces a hybrid CNN-Cellular Automata model for predicting spatially varying wildfire spread.
Develops an optimization framework to design effective, targeted aerial suppression strategies.
Addresses operational and environmental uncertainty inherent in real-world wildfire management.

stories.title: EvoArena: Tracking Memory Evolution for Robust LLM Agents in Dynamic Environments
stories.author: Zhiyuan Hu
bullet_points: Introduces EvoArena, a new benchmark suite designed to test LLM agents in dynamic environments.
Addresses a key limitation of current LLM evaluations, which often assume static operational environments.
Focuses on the necessity for agents to continuously adapt their knowledge and behavior to changing conditions.

stories.title: Learning to Reason by Analogy via Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Vicente Ordonez
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations of standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for complex reasoning tasks.
It proposes a novel framework called Retrieval-Augmented Reinforcement Fine-Tuning (RA-RFT).
RA-RFT aims to improve language model reasoning by moving beyond simple lexical or semantic similarity in knowledge retrieval.

stories.title: Influcoder: Distilling Decoders' Gradient Influence Rankings into an Encoder for Data Attribution
stories.author: Pascal Denis
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Influcoder,' a method to estimate data attribution (DA) in Large Language Models (LLMs).
DA methods aim to quantify how specific training samples influence a model's output, such as identifying sources of toxic behavior.
Influcoder achieves this by distilling gradient influence rankings from decoders into an encoder structure.

stories.title: HyperTool: Beyond Step-Wise Tool Calls for Tool-Augmented Agents
stories.author: Siheng Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the 'execution-granularity mismatch' in tool-augmented LLM agents.
Proposes 'HyperTool,' a unified, MCP-style tool interface.
Aims to move beyond traditional step-wise, atomic tool calls to improve context efficiency.

stories.title: Operadic consistency: a label-free signal for compositional reasoning failures in LLMs
stories.author: Kyle Richardson
bullet_points: Introduces 'Operadic consistency' as a novel, label-free diagnostic for identifying compositional reasoning failures in Large Language Models (LLMs).
This method leverages operad theory—a formalism for iterated substitution—to check if an LLM's direct answer matches the answer derived from a decomposed version of the same query.
It offers a complementary approach to existing confidence baselines (like self-consistency and semantic entropy) for evaluating LLM reliability during inference.

stories.title: SkMTEB: Slovak Massive Text Embedding Benchmark and Model Adaptation
stories.author: Viktória Ondrejová
bullet_points: Introduction of SkMTEB, the first comprehensive MTEB-style text embedding benchmark specifically for the low-resource Slovak language.
The benchmark covers 31 datasets across 7 task types, significantly expanding coverage compared to existing multilingual benchmarks for Slovak.
Findings indicate that large instruction-tuned multilingual models outperform existing Slovak-specific models when applied to text embedding tasks.

stories.title: repo-slopscore: Detecting AI/LLM contributions in git repositories via commit history analysis
stories.author: slopscan.ava.pet by ava
bullet_points: Introduces 'repo-slopscore,' a tool designed to detect contributions made by AI/LLMs within Git repositories.
The method relies on analyzing the commit history of the codebase to infer AI involvement.
The article is self-promotional, linking directly to the tool's source code and discussion thread.

stories.title: How to Keep AI Coding Agents from Hallucinating: A Guide to Harness Engineering
stories.author: masihmoafi
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of AI coding agent hallucination.
Proposes using a structured repository 'harness' as a solution.
Draws inspiration from Andrej Karpathy's established coding practices.

stories.title: Shouldn't AI Move From Cloud to Local Compute?
stories.author: js402
bullet_points: The article discusses the growing trend and necessity of shifting AI workloads from centralized cloud services to local, on-premise compute.
It touches upon practical implications related to existing tools like Copilot metering and APIs from providers like Anthropic.
The discussion highlights the role of hardware, specifically mentioning NVIDIA local AI boxes, in enabling self-controlled AI infrastructure.

stories.title: I Built 'Chat With Your Docs' From Scratch — Supabase + pgvector + a Free Local Embedder
stories.author: dev48v
bullet_points: The article details building a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system from scratch.
It leverages Supabase and pgvector for efficient document chunk retrieval.
The focus is on grounding LLM responses using retrieved context to minimize hallucinations.
